title: luscious rich Japanese potatoes Mochi
description: A resinous, dense, and kid-friendly Japanese dish from Hokkaido featuring potatoes, spherified for a unique texture, making it suitable for a satvik diet.
instructions: Combine 1 cup of water and 1/2 cup of sugar in a saucepan and heat until the sugar dissolves.
Wash and peel 2 large potatoes, then chop them into small cubes.
In a separate saucepan, mix 2 tablespoons of cornstarch with 1 cup of water to create a slurry.
Gradually add the slurry to the potato cubes and mix until the potatoes are coated.
Using a bamboo skewer, create small balls of the potato mixture and cook in a steamer for 4-5 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine 1/2 cup of sugar syrup and 1 teaspoon of matcha powder (if using) in a small bowl.
Once the potatoes are cooked, add them to the bowl and toss to coat with the matcha syrup.
Using a spherification kit or a syringe, create small spherical shapes with the potato mixture.
Paste the spheres with a pinch of edible gold dust (if using) and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Serve warm, garnished with sliced water chestnuts and a sprinkle of matcha powder (if using).
Optional: infuse the dish with green tea by drizzling a teaspoon of bottled green tea over the potatoes before serving.
Optional: add a pat of unsalted butter to the potatoes before serving for extra richness.
Garnish with a pinch of salt to taste.
